channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan and China propose five-point peace plan amid Iran conflict
item.description: Pakistan and China jointly called for an immediate ceasefire and early launch of peace negotiations to end the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran, stressing the need to prevent further escalation and safe...

item.title: 'Transcription' by Ben Lerner: Technology, Memory and the Intricacies of Human Connection
item.description: Ben Lerner's novel 'Transcription' weaves an intricate tapestry exploring the intersections of technology, memory, and human connection. The narrative follows a middle-aged American narrator who trave...

item.title: Trump urges other nations to secure oil in the Strait of Hormuz without US help
item.description: US President Donald Trump has suggested that nations not involved in the ongoing Middle East conflict should 'go get your own oil' in the Strait of Hormuz, as reported on his Truth Social account. He ...

item.title: Air Canada challenges $426K fine over passenger refund and rebooking violations during 2025 strike
item.description: Air Canada has announced it will contest a $426,000 fine imposed by the Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA) following 71 alleged violations of the Air Passenger Protection Regulations (APPR). These v...

item.title: Astronomers Observe Unprecedented Long-Lasting Gamma-Ray Burst with Webb Telescope
item.description: Astronomers have observed an extraordinary cosmic event, GRB 250702B, that challenges current understanding of stellar explosions. Detected by NASA's James Webb Space Telescope along with a network of...

item.title: TANTRADE officials visit Kwala Dry Port to observe operations and cargo handling
item.description: A group of 50 staff from Tanzania Trade Development Authority (TANTRADE), led by Deputy Director General Freddy Liundi, recently visited Kwala Dry Port in Vigwaza, Kibaha, Coast Region. The aim of the...

item.title: President Marcos mandates use of Philippine names for Kalayaan Island Group features
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has issued Executive Order No. 111, instructing that over 100 geographical features in the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG) in the West Philippine Sea be officially referred ...

item.title: Finance Minister Pledges Cooperation to KP Government Over Pending Financial Issues
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb met Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi on Tuesday and assured him of full support from the federal government in addressing all legitimate financial co...

item.title: Islamabad Police Arrest All Suspects in Businessman Murder During Robbery
item.description: On Tuesday, State Minister for Interior, Tallal Chaudhry, confirmed that Islamabad police have successfully apprehended all suspects involved in the killing of prominent businessman Amir Awan during a...

item.title: President Samia to attend charity to fund kidney and bone marrow transplants for children
item.description: In Dodoma, President Samia Suluhu Hassan is expected to be the guest of honour at a charity event aimed at helping 100 children with serious medical conditions receive life-saving treatments. The fund...

item.title: Government appoints Vir Vikram Yadav as DGCA Director General; Faiz Ahmed Kidwai shifts to DoPT
item.description: On 31 March 2026, the Government of India appointed senior IAS officer Vir Vikram Yadav as the new Director General of the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA), succeeding Faiz Ahmed Kidwai. T...

item.title: Unseasonably Low Temperatures Break Records Across British Columbia
item.description: On March 31, 2026, several communities across British Columbia experienced record-breaking cold temperatures, as reported by Environment Canada. The province, which typically transitions into milder s...

item.title: Tanzania’s Gemstone Industry Works to Improve Competitiveness Amid Global Market Pressure
item.description: Arusha, Tanzania – The Tanzanian gemstone sector is now facing stiff competition from international markets, particularly from countries like India, Thailand, and China, which have advanced technology...

item.title: Moderate Water Levels Recorded at Papanasam and Manimuthar Dams in Tirunelveli
item.description: As of Tuesday, the water level in the Papanasam Dam, located in Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu, measured 62.80 feet against its full reservoir capacity of 143 feet. The dam recorded an inflow of 1...

item.title: President Marcos pledges support for child facing harassment from local official
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. expressed strong concern after learning about an 11-year-old child who sought help against an allegedly abusive barangay kagawad in Camarines Sur. The President describe...

item.title: Jah Prayzah and Alaine to Perform in Harare for Afro Culture Concert
item.description: Zimbabwean music star Jah Prayzah and Jamaican reggae singer Alaine are set to headline a unique cultural concert titled 'Afro Culture Connect: A Tale of Two Cities' at the Harare International Confer...

item.title: Rising Collision Risks in Earth's Orbit and the Threat of Space Debris
item.description: Earth's orbit, once a vast empty expanse, has become increasingly congested since the launch of Sputnik in 1957. Initially hosting just a handful of satellites, orbital space now contains over 30,000 ...

item.title: Discussion on IOC Policy Restricting Female Sports Categories to Biological Women
item.description: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has recently announced a policy limiting participation in female sports categories to biological females, a move intended to ensure fairness and safety in com...

item.title: Africa Check finds no proof Ruto has dismissed aide Farouk Kibet
item.description: Africa Check has dismissed claims circulating on social media that Kenyan President William Ruto has expelled his long-time aide, Farouk Kibet. The viral story came from a memo allegedly signed by Rut...

item.title: Brandy Reflects on Her Career, Personal Struggles, and Musical Legacy in Memoir 'Phases'
item.description: Brandy’s memoir, Phases, offers an intimate look at her life and career, chronicling both triumphs and challenges. From her early years in Mississippi and California, where she developed her talent si...
